content.problem_latex:
To calculate the area of a rectangle with a length of $$7$$ and a breadth of $$5$$, we can use $$7\times 5$$. Therefore, the formula of a rectangle can be summarised as:．
(A) Area of rectangle $$=$$ breadth $$\times $$ breadth
(B) Area of rectangle $$=$$ length $$\times $$ length
(C) Area of rectangle $$=$$ length $$\times $$ breadth
content.problem_plain: To calculate the area of a rectangle with a length of 7 and a breadth of 5 , we can use 7 5 . Therefore, the formula of a rectangle can be summarised as:． (A) Area of rectangle = breadth breadth (B) Area of rectangle = length length (C) Area of rectangle = length breadth

content.problem_latex:
Jasson was selling some old stationery. A set of pens cost $\textbackslash$5x$ and a set of pencil cost $\textbackslash$y$. He then bought some new clothes for $\textbackslash$2x$. What is the amount of money Jasson has now in terms of $x$ and $y$?
(A) \textbackslash$(3x+y)
(B) \textbackslash$(3x-y)
(C) \textbackslash$(7x-y)
(D) \textbackslash$(7x+y)

content.problem_latex:
Expressions: The combination of numbers, symbols and operators.  (1) A single number or a single letter can be considered an algebraic expression.  (2) Equations and inequalities are NOT expressions.  Which of the following is \textbf{NOT} an expression?
(A) 3
(B) a
(C) a+b=a+b
(D) b+1+a
